project.title: Finance Knowledge Assistant — Multiformat RAG
project.blueprint_usage: Master source used to generate and validate the synthetic corpus. This file must not be indexed by the RAG system.

documents.title: Q3 Management Performance Report FY2025/26
documents.purpose: Narrative financial performance, business risks and Q4 priorities

documents.title: Finance Policies and Procedures
documents.purpose: Finance controls, approval requirements and month-end procedures

documents.title: Finance KPI Dictionary
documents.purpose: Definitions, formulas, ownership and targets for finance KPIs

documents.title: FP&A Forecast Meeting Notes — 10 April 2026
documents.purpose: Forecast commentary, decisions, actions, owners and deadlines

accounting_conventions.positive_expense_variance_meaning: Unfavourable — actual expense is above budget
accounting_conventions.negative_expense_variance_meaning: Favourable — actual expense is below budget
accounting_conventions.percentage_format: Rates are stored as decimal fractions. For example, 0.424 represents 42.4%.
accounting_conventions.rounding: Whole Australian dollars unless otherwise stated

variance_drivers.primary_cause: Delay in a planned vendor implementation milestone

business_risks.description: International freight costs may remain above budget during Q4.
business_risks.financial_impact: Pressure on gross margin and distribution expenses

business_risks.description: Late supplier deliveries may reduce availability of selected seasonal ranges.
business_risks.financial_impact: Lost sales and increased use of expedited freight

business_risks.description: Paid digital acquisition costs have increased faster than online conversion.
Wage cost pressure may increase store operating expenses.

q4_priorities.priority: Recover gross margin through tighter promotional controls and supplier negotiations.
Reduce slow-moving inventory and improve working capital.
Reallocate marketing spend toward channels with measurable conversion.
Complete the Q4 forecast refresh and identify actions to protect Adjusted EBITDA.

finance_policies.title: Month-end close timetable
rules: Departmental accrual submissions are due by 12:00 noon on business day 2.
Standard and recurring journals must be posted by the end of business day 3.
The general ledger must be closed by 17:00 on business day 5.
Late journals require approval from the Financial Controller.

finance_policies.title: Accrual recognition and review
rules: An accrual is required when goods or services have been received but the supplier invoice has not been recorded.
The minimum accrual threshold is AUD 5,000 per individual item.
Accruals must be supported by a purchase order, contract, supplier estimate or other reasonable calculation.
Accruals must be reviewed each month and reversed when the related invoice is recorded.

finance_policies.title: Prepayment recognition
rules: A payment must be recorded as a prepayment when it is at least AUD 12,000 and the benefit extends for more than three months.
Prepayments must be amortised monthly over the period in which the economic benefit is received.
The supporting schedule must show the supplier, total amount, service period and monthly release.

finance_policies.title: Purchase order requirements
rules: A purchase order must be approved before committing Harbour Retail Group to goods or services above AUD 5,000.
Splitting a purchase into smaller amounts to avoid the threshold is prohibited.
Payroll, taxes, rent, regulated utilities and emergency expenditure approved by the Finance Director are exempt.
Invoices without a valid purchase order may be returned to the requesting department.

finance_policies.title: Capital expenditure approval
additional_rules: Capital expenditure above AUD 50,000 requires a documented business case.
The business case must include expected benefits, implementation cost, timing, risks and financial return.
Projects may not be divided into smaller components to avoid an approval threshold.

finance_policies.title: Manual journal preparation and approval
rules: Every manual journal must include a clear description, calculation and supporting documentation.
The preparer and approver must be different people.
A journal preparer may not approve their own journal.
Journals above AUD 100,000 require approval from the Financial Controller.

finance_policies.title: Balance sheet reconciliation timetable
rules: All balance sheet accounts must be reconciled monthly.
Preparers must complete reconciliations by business day 7.
Reviewers must complete their review by business day 10.
Reconciling items older than 60 days must include an owner and a documented resolution date.

finance_policies.title: Finance document retention
rules: Accounting records and supporting documents must be retained for seven years.
Electronic records must be stored in an approved company system.
Documents subject to an audit, investigation or legal hold must not be destroyed.

finance_policies.title: Departmental forecast submissions
rules: Department Directors are responsible for the completeness and reasonableness of their forecasts.
Forecast submissions must explain material changes to revenue, expenditure, headcount and capital expenditure.
Material forecast movements above AUD 100,000 require written commentary.
The Head of FP&A consolidates and challenges departmental submissions before executive review.

kpi_dictionary.name: Revenue
kpi_dictionary.definition: Total income generated from the sale of goods before deducting operating expenses.
kpi_dictionary.formula: Gross sales less returns, discounts and sales taxes
kpi_dictionary.data_source: ERP general ledger and point-of-sale system

kpi_dictionary.name: Like-for-like sales growth
kpi_dictionary.definition: Percentage change in sales from comparable stores that traded in both the current and prior periods.
kpi_dictionary.formula: (Comparable store sales current period - comparable store sales prior period) / comparable store sales prior period

kpi_dictionary.name: Gross margin
kpi_dictionary.definition: Gross profit expressed as a percentage of revenue.

kpi_dictionary.name: Adjusted EBITDA
kpi_dictionary.definition: Earnings before interest, tax, depreciation and amortisation, adjusted for approved non-recurring items.
kpi_dictionary.formula: Operating profit + depreciation + amortisation + approved adjustments
kpi_dictionary.data_source: ERP general ledger and FP&A adjustment schedule

kpi_dictionary.name: Operating expense ratio
kpi_dictionary.definition: Operating expenses expressed as a percentage of revenue.

kpi_dictionary.name: Inventory days
kpi_dictionary.definition: Estimated number of days that inventory is held before being sold.
kpi_dictionary.formula: Average inventory / annualised cost of goods sold * 365
kpi_dictionary.data_source: ERP inventory and general ledger modules

kpi_dictionary.name: Stock availability
kpi_dictionary.definition: Percentage of active products available for sale when measured.
kpi_dictionary.formula: Available active product locations / total active product locations

kpi_dictionary.name: Online conversion rate
kpi_dictionary.definition: Percentage of website sessions that result in a completed customer order.
kpi_dictionary.data_source: E-commerce platform and web analytics system

kpi_dictionary.name: Average transaction value
kpi_dictionary.definition: Average revenue generated by each completed customer transaction.

kpi_dictionary.name: Labour cost percentage
kpi_dictionary.definition: Employee labour costs expressed as a percentage of revenue.
kpi_dictionary.data_source: Payroll system and ERP general ledger

kpi_dictionary.name: Forecast accuracy
kpi_dictionary.definition: Accuracy of the most recently approved forecast compared with actual results.
kpi_dictionary.data_source: FP&A planning model and ERP general ledger

kpi_dictionary.name: Net working capital
kpi_dictionary.definition: Short-term operating assets less short-term operating liabilities.

kpi_dictionary.name: Capital expenditure utilisation
kpi_dictionary.definition: Approved capital expenditure used or committed as a percentage of the annual capital budget.
kpi_dictionary.formula: (Capital expenditure incurred + committed expenditure) / approved capital budget
kpi_dictionary.data_source: ERP fixed asset module and capital expenditure register

forecast_meeting.title: FP&A Forecast Meeting
forecast_meeting.purpose: Review March performance, confirm the FY2025/26 outlook and agree actions to protect Q4 profitability and cash flow.

discussion_points.summary: March Marketing expenditure was AUD 185,000 above budget because of an unplanned digital customer acquisition campaign.
March Supply Chain expenditure was AUD 150,000 above budget because of freight surcharges and expedited shipments.
March Information Technology expenditure was AUD 45,000 below budget because a planned vendor implementation milestone was delayed.
E-commerce sales grew by 14.8%, but paid acquisition costs increased faster than online conversion.
Non-critical recruitment will be deferred until the Q4 forecast refresh confirms sufficient funding.

decisions.decision: No additional unplanned Marketing campaigns may begin without Finance Director approval.
The Q4 freight forecast will be updated using current surcharge rates and revised shipment assumptions.
The delayed IT implementation will remain in the forecast, but timing and cash flow assumptions must be refreshed.
Department Directors must identify additional Q4 savings before the executive forecast review.
Non-critical recruitment will be paused until the Q4 forecast refresh is approved.

action_items.action: Prepare a revised Marketing channel allocation showing expected spend, conversion and customer acquisition cost.
Update the Q4 logistics forecast using current freight surcharges and revised shipment volumes.
Refresh the IT implementation business case, timeline and cash flow profile.
Consolidate departmental savings and issue the updated Q4 forecast for executive review.
Prepare a slow-moving inventory reduction plan with category-level targets.
Provide an updated list of vacancies classified as critical or non-critical.
